definition: the condition of the air outside, such as temperature, rain, wind, or sun
example: The weather is warmer today than yesterday.
example2: I always check the weather before I leave home.
example3: The weather in Vancouver can change quickly.

definition: a report that says what the weather will probably be like
example: The forecast says it will rain this afternoon.
example2: I checked the forecast before planning the picnic.
example3: The weather forecast is usually helpful for commuters.

definition: how hot or cold something is
example: The temperature dropped last night.
example2: What is the temperature outside today?
example3: The temperature in my room is too high.

definition: a unit used to measure temperature
example: It is ten degrees outside this morning.
example2: The temperature may reach twenty-five degrees today.
example3: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.

definition: a scale used to measure temperature
example: In Canada, people usually use Celsius for weather.
example2: The forecast says it will be eighteen degrees Celsius.
example3: I am still learning how Celsius feels compared with Fahrenheit.

definition: a scale used to measure temperature, mainly used in the United States
example: Some American websites show the weather in Fahrenheit.
example2: I changed the app from Fahrenheit to Celsius.
example3: Thirty-two degrees Fahrenheit is zero degrees Celsius.

definition: bright with a lot of light from the sun
example: It is sunny today, so I will walk to the store.
example2: We had a sunny weekend in Surrey.
example3: A sunny day makes the park look beautiful.

definition: covered with clouds
example: It is cloudy, but it is not raining.
example2: The sky became cloudy in the afternoon.
example3: Cloudy weather is common in Vancouver.

definition: with a lot of rain
example: It was a rainy morning, so I took an umbrella.
example2: Rainy weather makes the roads slippery.
example3: I wear waterproof shoes on rainy days.

definition: with a lot of wind
example: It is windy near the water today.
example2: The windy weather made my umbrella turn inside out.
example3: Wear a jacket because it is windy outside.

definition: with strong wind, heavy rain, thunder, or lightning
example: The sky looks stormy tonight.
example2: Stormy weather can delay flights.
example3: We stayed inside during the stormy evening.

definition: with thick fog that makes it hard to see
example: It was foggy on the way to school.
example2: Drivers should slow down when it is foggy.
example3: The mountains disappeared in the foggy morning.

definition: with a lot of snow
example: The road was snowy after midnight.
example2: Snowy weather is not very common in Vancouver.
example3: Children like playing outside on snowy days.

definition: covered with ice or very cold
example: Be careful because the sidewalk is icy.
example2: The stairs were icy this morning.
example3: Icy roads can be dangerous for drivers.

definition: having a lot of water in the air
example: The air feels humid after the rain.
example2: It is humid today, so my clothes feel damp.
example3: Humid weather can make it hard to sleep.

definition: with little or no rain or moisture
example: The summer was warm and dry.
example2: My skin feels dry in cold weather.
example3: The forecast says the weekend will be dry.

definition: covered with water or rain
example: My shoes got wet in the rain.
example2: The grass is still wet this morning.
example3: Wet roads can be slippery.

definition: a little hot in a comfortable way
example: It is warm enough to wear a T-shirt.
example2: The afternoon became warm and sunny.
example3: I like warm weather in spring.

definition: having a high temperature
example: It is too hot to walk for a long time.
example2: Drink water when the weather is hot.
example3: The bus felt hot and crowded.

definition: a little cold in a comfortable way
example: The evening is cool near the ocean.
example2: Bring a light jacket because it may get cool.
example3: I enjoy cool weather for walking.

definition: having a low temperature
example: It is cold outside this morning.
example2: My hands get cold easily in winter.
example3: Wear a warm coat on cold days.

definition: extremely cold
example: It is freezing at the bus stop.
example2: The wind made the morning feel freezing.
example3: I need gloves when it is freezing outside.

definition: uncomfortably cool or a little cold
example: It is chilly, so I need a sweater.
example2: The classroom felt chilly today.
example3: A chilly wind came from the river.

definition: not very hot or very cold
example: Vancouver often has mild winters.
example2: The weather is mild today.
example3: A mild day is good for walking outside.

definition: a light wind
example: A cool breeze came through the window.
example2: There was a nice breeze at the beach.
example3: The breeze made the hot day more comfortable.

definition: moving air
example: The wind is strong today.
example2: I could hear the wind at night.
example3: The wind blew leaves across the street.

definition: a sudden strong wind
example: A gust of wind pushed the door open.
example2: The forecast warned about strong gusts.
example3: A sudden gust broke my umbrella.

definition: water that falls from the sky
example: The rain started after lunch.
example2: I like the sound of rain at night.
example3: Heavy rain made traffic slower.

definition: a short period of rain
example: There may be a shower in the afternoon.
example2: A quick shower passed through the city.
example3: Bring an umbrella because showers are possible.

definition: very light rain
example: A light drizzle started this morning.
example2: The drizzle made my glasses wet.
example3: Vancouver often has drizzle in the fall.

definition: a sudden heavy rain
example: We waited inside during the downpour.
example2: The downpour flooded part of the street.
example3: A downpour began while I was walking home.

definition: very bad weather with strong wind, rain, snow, thunder, or lightning
example: The storm lasted all night.
example2: A storm may arrive this evening.
example3: The storm caused power problems in some areas.

definition: a storm with thunder and lightning
example: A thunderstorm is possible tonight.
example2: The thunderstorm scared the dog.
example3: Flights can be delayed during a thunderstorm.

definition: the loud sound after lightning
example: I heard thunder in the distance.
example2: The thunder was very loud last night.
example3: Thunder usually comes after lightning.

definition: a bright flash of electricity in the sky
example: Lightning lit up the dark sky.
example2: Stay inside when there is lightning.
example3: I saw lightning over the mountains.

definition: soft white pieces of frozen water that fall from the sky
example: Snow covered the cars overnight.
example2: The forecast says snow may fall tomorrow.
example3: I have never driven in heavy snow.

definition: a small piece of falling snow
example: A snowflake landed on my jacket.
example2: Each snowflake has a different shape.
example3: Children watched the snowflakes fall.

definition: small hard balls of ice that fall from the sky
example: Hail hit the window loudly.
example2: The hail stopped after a few minutes.
example3: Do not stand outside during heavy hail.

definition: rain mixed with snow or ice
example: The sleet made the sidewalk slippery.
example2: The forecast warns of sleet tonight.
example3: Sleet can make driving difficult.

definition: frozen water
example: There is ice on the windshield.
example2: The puddle turned into ice overnight.
example3: Be careful of ice near the bus stop.

definition: thin white ice that forms on cold surfaces
example: There was frost on the grass this morning.
example2: I had to scrape frost off the car window.
example3: Frost can damage some plants.

definition: thick cloud close to the ground that makes it hard to see
example: Fog covered the bridge in the morning.
example2: The fog made the road hard to see.
example3: The fog slowly disappeared after sunrise.

definition: thin fog or very small drops of water in the air
example: A light mist covered the lake.
example2: The mountains looked beautiful in the mist.
example3: Mist made the air feel cool.

definition: a white or gray mass in the sky made of tiny drops of water
example: A dark cloud moved across the sky.
example2: There were no clouds this morning.
example3: The clouds looked heavy before the rain.

definition: the space above the earth that you see when you look up
example: The sky is blue today.
example2: The sky turned gray before the storm.
example3: I saw birds flying across the sky.

definition: the bright star that gives the earth light and heat
example: The sun came out after the rain.
example2: The sun feels strong today.
example3: We sat in the sun during lunch.

definition: light from the sun
example: I enjoyed the sunshine at the park.
example2: The room filled with sunshine in the morning.
example3: After many rainy days, the sunshine felt nice.

definition: an area where direct sunlight is blocked
example: We sat in the shade to stay cool.
example2: The tree gives good shade in summer.
example3: Find some shade if the sun is too strong.

definition: a curved line of colors in the sky after rain
example: We saw a rainbow after the shower.
example2: The rainbow appeared over the city.
example3: Children pointed at the rainbow in the sky.

definition: the time when the sun appears in the morning
example: Sunrise was beautiful today.
example2: I left home before sunrise.
example3: The sky was pink at sunrise.

definition: the time when the sun goes down in the evening
example: We watched the sunset at the beach.
example2: The sunset made the sky orange.
example3: I took a photo of the sunset.

definition: covered with clouds and not bright
example: The sky is overcast today.
example2: It was overcast all morning.
example3: Overcast weather can make the day feel dark.

definition: with some clouds and some clear sky
example: The forecast says it will be partly cloudy.
example2: It was partly cloudy during our walk.
example3: A partly cloudy day can still be pleasant.

definition: without clouds, fog, or rain
example: The sky is clear tonight.
example2: We had a clear view of the mountains.
example3: Clear weather is good for driving.

definition: full of light
example: It is bright outside, so I need sunglasses.
example2: The bright morning made me feel awake.
example3: The snow looked bright in the sun.

definition: with little or no light
example: The sky became dark before the storm.
example2: It gets dark early in winter.
example3: Dark clouds usually mean rain is coming.

definition: how clean or dirty the air is
example: The air quality is poor because of smoke.
example2: I check the air quality before running outside.
example3: Good air quality makes breathing easier.

definition: gray or black air from fire
example: Smoke from wildfires affected the city.
example2: The sky looked orange because of smoke.
example3: Stay indoors when there is heavy smoke.

definition: a large fire that spreads in a forest or grass area
example: Wildfire smoke can travel far.
example2: The news reported a wildfire north of the city.
example3: Hot dry weather can increase wildfire risk.

definition: a period of unusually hot weather
example: A heat wave is expected next week.
example2: Drink more water during a heat wave.
example3: The city opened cooling centres during the heat wave.

definition: a short period of very cold weather
example: A cold snap arrived in January.
example2: The cold snap made the roads icy.
example3: Plants can die during a cold snap.

definition: the amount of water in the air
example: The humidity is high today.
example2: High humidity makes the air feel heavy.
example3: My weather app shows the humidity level.

definition: the force of the air in the atmosphere
example: Air pressure can change before a storm.
example2: The weather report mentioned low pressure.
example3: Some people get headaches when pressure changes.

definition: how far you can see
example: Visibility was low because of fog.
example2: Drivers need to be careful when visibility is poor.
example3: Heavy rain reduced visibility on the highway.

definition: water that falls from the sky, such as rain, snow, or hail
example: The forecast shows a chance of precipitation.
example2: Precipitation is common in winter here.
example3: Snow is one type of precipitation.

definition: the possibility that it will rain
example: There is a fifty percent chance of rain today.
example2: I bring an umbrella when there is a chance of rain.
example3: The chance of rain is higher in the evening.

definition: a number that shows how strong the sun's ultraviolet light is
example: The UV index is high today.
example2: Use sunscreen when the UV index is high.
example3: I check the UV index before going to the beach.

definition: cream or spray that protects your skin from the sun
example: I put on sunscreen before going outside.
example2: You should wear sunscreen on sunny days.
example3: Sunscreen helps prevent sunburn.

definition: red painful skin caused by too much sun
example: I got a sunburn at the beach.
example2: Wear a hat to avoid sunburn.
example3: My shoulders hurt because of sunburn.

definition: an object you hold over your head to protect yourself from rain or sun
example: I forgot my umbrella at home.
example2: Many people carry umbrellas in Vancouver.
example3: My umbrella broke in the wind.

definition: a coat that keeps you dry in the rain
example: I wear a raincoat on wet days.
example2: A good raincoat is useful in Canada.
example3: Her raincoat kept her clothes dry.

definition: not allowing water to pass through
example: These shoes are waterproof.
example2: I need a waterproof jacket for rainy weather.
example3: A waterproof bag protects my books.

definition: strong shoes that cover the foot and part of the leg
example: I wear boots when it rains heavily.
example2: Snow boots keep my feet warm.
example3: Her boots got muddy after the walk.

definition: clothing worn on the hands
example: I need gloves because my hands are cold.
example2: Warm gloves are useful in winter.
example3: I lost one of my gloves on the bus.

definition: a piece of cloth worn around the neck for warmth
example: She wore a scarf on the chilly morning.
example2: A scarf can protect your neck from wind.
example3: I bought a warm scarf for winter.

definition: a warm piece of clothing worn outside
example: I need a coat today because it is cold.
example2: Hang your wet coat near the door.
example3: A winter coat is important in Canada.

definition: a short coat
example: Bring a jacket because it may get windy.
example2: I wore a light jacket in the evening.
example3: My jacket has a hood for the rain.

definition: several pieces of clothing worn one over another
example: Wear layers because the weather may change.
example2: Layers help you stay warm outside.
example3: I removed one layer when the room got hot.

definition: the part of a coat or jacket that covers your head
example: Put up your hood when it rains.
example2: My jacket has a waterproof hood.
example3: The wind blew my hood off.

definition: a small pool of water on the ground
example: I stepped in a puddle by accident.
example2: There were puddles after the rain.
example3: Children jumped in the puddles.

definition: difficult to stand or walk on because it is wet or icy
example: The sidewalk is slippery after the snow.
example2: Be careful on slippery stairs.
example3: Wet leaves can make the path slippery.

definition: the regular trip between home and work or school
example: Bad weather can make my commute longer.
example2: My commute is harder on rainy days.
example3: I check transit alerts before my morning commute.

definition: a time when something happens later than planned
example: There was a bus delay because of snow.
example2: Weather delays are common during storms.
example3: The delay made me late for class.

definition: information that tells people about possible danger
example: The weather warning said heavy rain was coming.
example2: I received a wind warning on my phone.
example3: Warnings help people prepare for bad weather.

definition: a message that tells people about danger or important information
example: My phone sent a weather alert.
example2: The alert warned people about icy roads.
example3: Check alerts before traveling in a storm.

definition: official advice or information about a possible problem
example: There is a weather advisory for heavy rain.
example2: The city issued a travel advisory.
example3: I read the advisory before driving.

definition: a large amount of water covering land that is usually dry
example: Heavy rain can cause a flood.
example2: The road was closed because of a flood.
example3: Do not walk through flood water.

definition: a time when electricity stops working
example: The storm caused a power outage.
example2: We used flashlights during the power outage.
example3: A power outage can happen during strong winds.

definition: thin clear ice on a road or sidewalk that is hard to see
example: Black ice is dangerous for drivers.
example2: Be careful because there may be black ice tonight.
example3: The road looked dry, but it had black ice.

definition: how cold the air feels because of wind
example: The wind chill makes it feel much colder.
example2: Check the wind chill before going outside.
example3: The temperature is minus two, but the wind chill feels like minus eight.

definition: used to say how hot or cold the weather seems to your body
example: It is ten degrees, but it feels like five.
example2: The app says it feels like thirty degrees.
example3: Wind can change how cold it feels like outside.

definition: the highest temperature of the day
example: Today’s high will be twenty degrees.
example2: The daily high is warmer than usual.
example3: Tomorrow’s high may reach twenty-five.

definition: the lowest temperature of the day
example: Tonight’s low will be five degrees.
example2: The overnight low is close to freezing.
example3: Check the low before choosing your clothes.

definition: happening every day
example: I check the daily forecast every morning.
example2: The daily temperature changes a lot in spring.
example3: Daily weather can affect my plans.

definition: happening or shown for each hour
example: The hourly forecast shows rain at 3 p.m.
example2: I use the hourly weather report before leaving school.
example3: Hourly updates are useful on stormy days.

definition: one of the four parts of the year: spring, summer, fall, or winter
example: Winter is the coldest season in Canada.
example2: My favorite season is fall.
example3: Each season has different weather.

definition: the season after winter and before summer
example: Spring weather can be rainy and mild.
example2: Flowers grow in spring.
example3: I wear a light jacket in spring.

definition: the hottest season of the year
example: Summer days are long and warm.
example2: Many people go to the beach in summer.
example3: The weather is usually drier in summer.

definition: the season after summer and before winter
example: Fall is often cool and rainy.
example2: Leaves change color in fall.
example3: I bought a new coat for fall.

definition: the coldest season of the year
example: Winter nights are long and cold.
example2: It may snow in winter.
example3: I need warm clothes for winter.
